question.text: Under the Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS) governed by FEMA 1999, what is the maximum amount an Indian resident individual can remit abroad per financial year?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS) governed by FEMA 1999, Indian resident individuals can remit up to USD 250,000 per financial year for permissible capital and current account transactions without prior RBI approval. **The Trap:** USD 100,000 and USD 150,000 are common distractors because candidates often underestimate the LRS limit. USD 500,000 is too high and was never the prescribed limit. **Cross-Exam Context:** LRS limits are tested in NISM Series V-A (MF Distributors), Series X-A, and Series X-B exams, as well as in NISM Currency Derivatives certification. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always verify the current LRS limit before advising clients on overseas investments — this limit is reviewed periodically by RBI and could change based on macroeconomic conditions.
